docs.title: Google MCP Server
docs.text: An MCP (Model Context Protocol) server for reading Google Slides presentations and Google Docs documents.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Read-only access to Google Slides and Google Docs via MCP tools
  • 9 tools for comprehensive document access
  • Composable architecture built on omniskill
  • Multiple credential options including vault-backed credentials via omnitoken

docs.title: Google Slides Tools
docs.text: Tool Description get_presentation Get presentation metadata (title, slide count, locale, revision ID) list_slides List all slides with titles and element counts get_slide Get slide content and element details by index or object ID get_slide_notes Get speaker notes by slide index or object ID get_presentation_content Get all slides' text and images in one call (ideal for AI)

docs.title: Google Docs Tools
docs.text: Tool Description get_document Get document metadata (title, word count, element counts) get_document_content Get structured content (headings, paragraphs, images, tables) get_document_text Get all text as a single plain text string get_document_paragraphs Get text organized by paragraphs

docs.title: Why Composable?
docs.text:
This server is built on omniskill, making its Google Slides and Docs skills reusable building blocks. You can:

  1. Use standalone - Run mcp-google as a focused Google tools server
  2. Compose with others - Import the skills into a multi-service MCP server combining Google + Slack + Jira + GitHub
import (
    "github.com/grokify/mcp-google/skills/slides"
    "github.com/grokify/mcp-google/skills/docs"
)

// Add to any omniskill-based server
rt.RegisterSkill(slides.New(httpClient))
rt.RegisterSkill(docs.New(httpClient))

See Architecture for more details.

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
# Install
go install github.com/grokify/mcp-google/cmd/mcp-google@latest

# Run with Google service account
mcp-google --credentials /path/to/service-account.json

See Getting Started for full setup instructions.

docs.title: Composability
docs.text: One of the key design goals of Google MCP Server is composability - the ability to combine its skills with others to create multi-service MCP servers.

docs.title: Traditional Approach (Monolithic)
docs.text:
Without composability, you end up with many separate MCP servers:

┌─────────────────┐  ┌─────────────────┐  ┌─────────────────┐
│ google-mcp-srv  │  │ slack-mcp-srv   │  │ jira-mcp-srv    │
│   9 tools       │  │   12 tools      │  │   8 tools       │
└─────────────────┘  └─────────────────┘  └─────────────────┘
         │                   │                    │
         └───────────┬───────┴────────────────────┘
                     │
              ┌──────▼──────┐
              │  MCP Client │
              │ (3 servers) │
              └─────────────┘

Problems:

  • Multiple processes to manage
  • Multiple connections for the client
  • Harder configuration
  • Resource overhead

docs.title: Composable Approach
docs.text:
With omniskill composability, you can build one unified server:

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              work-mcp-server                     │
│  ┌─────────┐ ┌─────────┐ ┌─────────┐ ┌────────┐ │
│  │ slides  │ │  docs   │ │  slack  │ │  jira  │ │
│  └─────────┘ └─────────┘ └─────────┘ └────────┘ │
│                    29 tools                      │
└─────────────────────┬───────────────────────────┘
                      │
               ┌──────▼──────┐
               │  MCP Client │
               │ (1 server)  │
               └─────────────┘

Benefits:

  • Single process
  • Single connection
  • Unified configuration
  • Lower resource usage

docs.title: Step 1: Import Skills
docs.text:
import (
    "github.com/grokify/mcp-google/skills/slides"
    "github.com/grokify/mcp-google/skills/docs"
    // Skills from other repos
    "github.com/example/slack-mcp-skills/skills/messages"
    "github.com/example/jira-mcp-skills/skills/issues"
)

docs.title: Step 2: Create Authentication Clients
docs.text:
Each service needs its own authenticated client:

// Google auth (shared between slides and docs)
googleClient, err := getGoogleHTTPClient(ctx)

// Slack auth
slackClient, err := getSlackHTTPClient(ctx)

// Jira auth
jiraClient, err := getJiraHTTPClient(ctx)

docs.title: Step 3: Initialize Skills
docs.text:
slidesSkill := slides.New(googleClient)
slidesSkill.Init(ctx)

docsSkill := docs.New(googleClient)
docsSkill.Init(ctx)

slackSkill := messages.New(slackClient)
slackSkill.Init(ctx)

jiraSkill := issues.New(jiraClient)
jiraSkill.Init(ctx)

docs.title: Step 4: Register with Runtime
docs.text:
rt := runtime.New(&mcp.Implementation{
    Name:    "work-mcp-server",
    Version: "v1.0.0",
}, nil)

// Register all skills
rt.RegisterSkill(slidesSkill)
rt.RegisterSkill(docsSkill)
rt.RegisterSkill(slackSkill)
rt.RegisterSkill(jiraSkill)

// Serve
rt.ServeStdio(ctx)

docs.title: Complete Example: Multi-Service Server
docs.text:
package main

import (
    "context"
    "log"
    "os"

    "github.com/grokify/mcp-google/skills/slides"
    "github.com/grokify/mcp-google/skills/docs"
    "github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk/mcp"
    runtime "github.com/plexusone/omniskill/mcp/server"
)

func main() {
    ctx := context.Background()

    // Get authenticated clients
    googleClient := getGoogleClient(ctx)

    // Initialize skills
    slidesSkill := slides.New(googleClient)
    if err := slidesSkill.Init(ctx); err != nil {
        log.Fatal(err)
    }
    defer slidesSkill.Close()

    docsSkill := docs.New(googleClient)
    if err := docsSkill.Init(ctx); err != nil {
        log.Fatal(err)
    }
    defer docsSkill.Close()

    // Create runtime
    rt := runtime.New(&mcp.Implementation{
        Name:    "work-mcp-server",
        Version: "v1.0.0",
    }, nil)

    // Register skills (use prefix to avoid conflicts)
    rt.RegisterSkillWithPrefix(slidesSkill)  // slides_get_presentation, etc.
    rt.RegisterSkillWithPrefix(docsSkill)    // docs_get_document, etc.

    // Run
    if err := rt.ServeStdio(ctx); err != nil {
        log.Fatal(err)
    }
}

docs.title: 1. Use Prefixes When Combining
docs.text:
When combining multiple skills, use RegisterSkillWithPrefix() to avoid tool name conflicts:

rt.RegisterSkillWithPrefix(slidesSkill)  // slides_*
rt.RegisterSkillWithPrefix(docsSkill)    // docs_*

docs.title: 2. Share Authentication Where Possible
docs.text:
Google Slides and Docs can share the same authenticated HTTP client:

googleClient := getGoogleHTTPClient(ctx)
slidesSkill := slides.New(googleClient)
docsSkill := docs.New(googleClient)

docs.title: 3. Proper Cleanup
docs.text:
Always defer Close() for each skill:

defer slidesSkill.Close()
defer docsSkill.Close()

docs.title: 4. Handle Initialization Errors
docs.text:
Skills may fail to initialize (network issues, invalid credentials):

if err := slidesSkill.Init(ctx); err != nil {
    log.Fatalf("Failed to initialize Slides skill: %v", err)
}

docs.title: OmniSkill Integration
docs.text: OmniSkill is a Go framework for building composable MCP servers. Google MCP Server uses omniskill to provide a modular, reusable architecture.

docs.title: What is OmniSkill?
docs.text:
OmniSkill provides:

  • Skill interface: A standard way to package MCP tools
  • Runtime: MCP server with multiple transport options (stdio, HTTP, SSE)
  • Registry: Skill discovery and management
  • Library mode: Direct invocation without MCP protocol overhead

docs.title: Skill Interface
docs.text:
Each Google skill implements the skill.Skill interface:

type Skill interface {
    Name() string                    // Skill identifier
    Description() string             // Human-readable description
    Tools() []Tool                   // List of tools
    Init(ctx context.Context) error  // Initialize before use
    Close() error                    // Cleanup on shutdown
}

docs.title: Slides Skill
docs.text:
package slides

type Skill struct {
    httpClient    *http.Client
    presentations *gslides.PresentationsService
}

func New(httpClient *http.Client) *Skill {
    return &Skill{httpClient: httpClient}
}

func (s *Skill) Name() string { return "slides" }

func (s *Skill) Tools() []skill.Tool {
    return []skill.Tool{
        s.getPresentationTool(),
        s.listSlidesTool(),
        s.getSlideTool(),
        s.getSlideNotesTool(),
        s.getPresentationContentTool(),
    }
}

func (s *Skill) Init(ctx context.Context) error {
    svc, err := gslides.NewService(ctx, option.WithHTTPClient(s.httpClient))
    if err != nil {
        return err
    }
    s.presentations = gslides.NewPresentationsService(svc)
    return nil
}

docs.title: Docs Skill
docs.text:
package docs

type Skill struct {
    httpClient *http.Client
    documents  *docs.DocumentsService
}

func New(httpClient *http.Client) *Skill {
    return &Skill{httpClient: httpClient}
}

func (s *Skill) Name() string { return "docs" }

func (s *Skill) Tools() []skill.Tool {
    return []skill.Tool{
        s.getDocumentTool(),
        s.getDocumentContentTool(),
        s.getDocumentTextTool(),
        s.getDocumentParagraphsTool(),
    }
}

docs.title: Tool Definition
docs.text:
Tools use skill.NewTool() for clean definitions:

func (s *Skill) getDocumentTool() skill.Tool {
    return skill.NewTool(
        "get_document",
        "Get metadata about a Google Doc document",
        map[string]skill.Parameter{
            "document_id": {
                Type:        "string",
                Description: "The ID or URL of the Google Doc",
                Required:    true,
            },
        },
        func(ctx context.Context, params map[string]any) (any, error) {
            documentID := params["document_id"].(string)
            // ... implementation
            return result, nil
        },
    )
}

docs.title: Runtime Usage
docs.text:
The main server creates an omniskill Runtime and registers skills:

import runtime "github.com/plexusone/omniskill/mcp/server"

// Create runtime
rt := runtime.New(&mcp.Implementation{
    Name:    "mcp-google",
    Version: "v0.4.0",
}, nil)

// Initialize and register skills
slidesSkill := slides.New(httpClient)
slidesSkill.Init(ctx)
rt.RegisterSkill(slidesSkill)

docsSkill := docs.New(httpClient)
docsSkill.Init(ctx)
rt.RegisterSkill(docsSkill)

// Serve via stdio
rt.ServeStdio(ctx)

docs.title: Without Prefix (Default)
docs.text:
Tools are registered with their original names:

rt.RegisterSkill(slidesSkill)
// Tools: get_presentation, list_slides, get_slide, etc.

docs.title: With Prefix
docs.text:
Tools are prefixed with skill name to avoid conflicts:

rt.RegisterSkillWithPrefix(slidesSkill)
// Tools: slides_get_presentation, slides_list_slides, etc.

This is useful when combining skills that might have tool name conflicts.

docs.title: Benefits of OmniSkill
docs.text:
1. Composability: Skills can be imported into any omniskill-based server
  2. Standardization: Common interface for all skills
  3. Transport flexibility: stdio, HTTP, SSE without code changes
  4. Library mode: Call tools directly without MCP protocol
  5. Registry: Centralized skill discovery

docs.title: Architecture Overview
docs.text: Google MCP Server is built on a composable architecture using omniskill, enabling its Google skills to be reused in other MCP servers.

docs.title: 1. Composability First
docs.text:
Rather than being a monolithic MCP server, this project exposes its functionality as skills - modular units that can be:

  • Used standalone in mcp-google
  • Imported into other omniskill-based servers
  • Combined with skills from other services (Slack, Jira, GitHub, etc.)

docs.title: 2. Separation of Concerns
docs.text:
mcp-google/
├── cmd/mcp-google/    # CLI entry point
├── skills/                   # Exportable omniskill modules
│   ├── slides/              # Google Slides skill
│   └── docs/                # Google Docs skill
└── internal/
    └── auth/                # Shared authentication
  • Skills: Contain all business logic, tools, and API interactions
  • CLI: Only handles configuration and skill orchestration
  • Auth: Shared authentication layer using omnitoken

docs.title: 3. Skill Independence
docs.text:
Each skill is self-contained:

  • Has its own Init() lifecycle
  • Manages its own Google API client
  • Defines its own tools
  • Can be registered independently

docs.title: Key Dependencies
docs.text: Package Purpose omniskill Skill framework and MCP runtime omnitoken Token management with vault backends gogoogle Google API utilities goauth Authentication utilities

docs.title: Data Flow
docs.text:
1. Initialization:
  2. CLI parses credentials configuration
  3. omnitoken creates authenticated HTTP client
  4. Skills are initialized with the HTTP client

  5. Skills register with omniskill Runtime

  6. Request Handling:

  7. MCP client sends tool call request
  8. omniskill Runtime routes to appropriate skill
  9. Skill executes tool logic using Google APIs

  10. Response returned via MCP protocol

  11. Shutdown:

  12. Skills Close() called for cleanup
  13. Token manager releases resources

docs.title: Claude Desktop Configuration
docs.text: Configure Claude Desktop to use Google MCP Server.

docs.title: Configuration File Location
docs.text: OS Path macOS ~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json Windows %APPDATA%\Claude\claude_desktop_config.json Linux ~/.config/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json

docs.title: With Google Service Account
docs.text:
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "google": {
      "command": "/path/to/mcp-google",
      "args": ["--credentials", "/path/to/service-account.json"]
    }
  }
}

docs.title: With goauth
docs.text:
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "google": {
      "command": "/path/to/mcp-google",
      "args": [
        "--goauth-credentials-file", "/path/to/credentials.json",
        "--goauth-credentials-account", "myaccount"
      ]
    }
  }
}

docs.title: With File Vault
docs.text:
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "google": {
      "command": "/path/to/mcp-google",
      "args": [
        "--vault", "file:///path/to/secrets",
        "--credentials-name", "google"
      ]
    }
  }
}

docs.title: With 1Password
docs.text:
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "google": {
      "command": "/path/to/mcp-google",
      "env": {
        "OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN": "ops_...",
        "OMNITOKEN_VAULT_URI": "op://MyVault",
        "OMNITOKEN_CREDENTIALS_NAME": "google"
      }
    }
  }
}

docs.title: With Bitwarden
docs.text:
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "google": {
      "command": "/path/to/mcp-google",
      "env": {
        "BW_ACCESS_TOKEN": "...",
        "BW_ORGANIZATION_ID": "...",
        "OMNITOKEN_VAULT_URI": "bw://org-id",
        "OMNITOKEN_CREDENTIALS_NAME": "google"
      }
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Google Service Account
docs.text:
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "google": {
      "command": "/path/to/mcp-google",
      "env": {
        "GOOGLE_CREDENTIALS_FILE": "/path/to/service-account.json"
      }
    }
  }
}

docs.title: goauth CredentialsSet
docs.text:
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "google": {
      "command": "/path/to/mcp-google",
      "env": {
        "GOAUTH_CREDENTIALS_FILE": "/path/to/credentials.json",
        "GOAUTH_CREDENTIALS_ACCOUNT": "google-slides"
      }
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Environment Variables Reference
docs.text: Variable Description GOOGLE_CREDENTIALS_FILE Path to Google service account JSON GOAUTH_CREDENTIALS_FILE Path to goauth CredentialsSet JSON GOAUTH_CREDENTIALS_ACCOUNT Account key in goauth file OMNITOKEN_VAULT_URI Vault URI (e.g., op://MyVault, bw://org-id) OMNITOKEN_CREDENTIALS_NAME Credential name in vault (default: google) OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN 1Password service account token BW_ACCESS_TOKEN Bitwarden access token BW_ORGANIZATION_ID Bitwarden organization ID

docs.title: Multiple Servers
docs.text:
You can run multiple MCP servers alongside Google:

{
  "mcpServers": {
    "google": {
      "command": "/path/to/mcp-google",
      "args": ["--credentials", "/path/to/google-service-account.json"]
    },
    "github": {
      "command": "/path/to/github-mcp-server",
      "args": ["--token", "ghp_xxx"]
    },
    "filesystem": {
      "command": "/path/to/filesystem-mcp-server",
      "args": ["--root", "/home/user/documents"]
    }
  }
}

docs.title: If installed with go install
docs.text:
# Find GOPATH
go env GOPATH

# Binary is at $GOPATH/bin/mcp-google
# Typically: ~/go/bin/mcp-google

docs.title: If built from source
docs.text:
Use the full path to where you built it:

/path/to/mcp-google/mcp-google

docs.title: Server Not Starting
docs.text:
Check the Claude Desktop logs:

  • macOS: ~/Library/Logs/Claude/
  • Windows: %APPDATA%\Claude\logs\

Common issues:

  1. Binary not found: Verify the path is correct
  2. Credentials not found: Check the credentials file path
  3. Permission denied: Ensure the binary is executable (chmod +x)

docs.title: Verifying Configuration
docs.text:
Test the server manually:

# Should start and wait for input (Ctrl+C to exit)
/path/to/mcp-google --credentials /path/to/creds.json

docs.title: JSON Syntax Errors
docs.text:
Validate your JSON:

# On macOS/Linux
cat ~/Library/Application\ Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json | python3 -m json.tool

docs.title: Available Tools in Claude
docs.text:
Once configured, you can ask Claude to:

  • "Read the Google Slides presentation at [URL]"
  • "Get the text from this Google Doc: [URL or ID]"
  • "List all slides in presentation [ID]"
  • "Show me the speaker notes for slide 3"

Claude will use the appropriate tools:

  • get_presentation / get_presentation_content for Slides
  • get_document / get_document_text for Docs

docs.title: Credentials
docs.text: Google MCP Server supports multiple credential sources for authentication.

docs.title: Option 1: Google Service Account
docs.text: The simplest option - use a standard Google Cloud service account JSON file.

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
1. Create a service account in Google Cloud Console
  2. Download the JSON credentials file
  3. Share documents with the service account email

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: mcp-google --credentials /path/to/service-account.json

docs.title: JSON Format
docs.text:
{
  "type": "service_account",
  "project_id": "your-project",
  "private_key_id": "key-id",
  "private_key": "-----BEGIN PRIVATE KEY-----\n...\n-----END PRIVATE KEY-----\n",
  "client_email": "mcp-server@your-project.iam.gserviceaccount.com",
  "client_id": "123456789",
  "auth_uri": "https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/auth",
  "token_uri": "https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token"
}

docs.title: Option 2: goauth CredentialsSet
docs.text: Use a goauth CredentialsSet file for managing multiple credentials.

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
Create a CredentialsSet JSON file with your Google credentials:

{
  "credentials": {
    "myaccount": {
      "type": "gcpsa",
      "gcpsa": {
        "gcpCredentials": {
          "type": "service_account",
          "project_id": "your-project",
          "private_key_id": "key-id",
          "private_key": "-----BEGIN PRIVATE KEY-----\n...\n-----END PRIVATE KEY-----\n",
          "client_email": "mcp-server@your-project.iam.gserviceaccount.com",
          "client_id": "123456789"
        },
        "scopes": [
          "https://www.googleapis.com/auth/presentations.readonly",
          "https://www.googleapis.com/auth/documents.readonly",
          "https://www.googleapis.com/auth/drive.readonly"
        ]
      }
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
mcp-google \
  --goauth-credentials-file /path/to/credentials.json \
  --goauth-credentials-account myaccount

docs.title: Benefits
docs.text:
• Store multiple accounts in one file
  • Pre-configure scopes
  • Consistent credential format across tools

docs.title: Option 3: Vault-Backed Credentials
docs.text: Use omnitoken with vault backends for secure credential storage.

docs.title: Supported Vault URIs
docs.text: URI Pattern Description Requirements op://vault 1Password OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN env var bw://org-id Bitwarden BW_ACCESS_TOKEN and BW_ORGANIZATION_ID env vars file:///path/to/dir File-based storage None env://PREFIX_ Environment variables with prefix None memory:// In-memory (testing only) None

docs.title: 1Password
docs.text:
Store your goauth credentials in 1Password and access them securely:

# Set 1Password service account token
export OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN="ops_..."

# Use 1Password vault
mcp-google --vault op://MyVault --credentials-name google

The credential item in 1Password should contain the goauth Credentials JSON in a field.

docs.title: Bitwarden
docs.text:
Store credentials in Bitwarden Secrets Manager:

# Set Bitwarden credentials
export BW_ACCESS_TOKEN="..."
export BW_ORGANIZATION_ID="..."

# Use Bitwarden vault
mcp-google --vault bw://org-id --credentials-name google

docs.title: File Vault
docs.text: mcp-google --vault file:///path/to/secrets --credentials-name google

docs.title: File Vault Structure
docs.text:
/path/to/secrets/
└── google.json    # Contains goauth Credentials JSON

docs.title: Environment Vault
docs.text:
# With env:// vault URI, credentials are read from environment variables
export GOOGLE_CREDENTIALS='{"type":"gcpsa",...}'
mcp-google --vault env://GOOGLE_ --credentials-name CREDENTIALS

docs.title: Credential Priority
docs.text: If multiple options are specified, the server returns an error. Only one credential source is allowed.

docs.title: Recommended Approach
docs.text: Use Case Recommended Option Local development Google Service Account Multiple Google accounts goauth CredentialsSet Production with secrets management Vault-backed Combining with other services Vault-backed

docs.title: Security Best Practices
docs.text:
1. Never commit credentials - Add credentials files to .gitignore
  2. Use file permissions - chmod 600 service-account.json
  3. Principle of least privilege - Only request needed scopes
  4. Rotate keys - Periodically rotate service account keys
  5. Use vault backends - For production, use proper secrets management

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text: All command-line flags can be set via environment variables.

docs.title: Credential Configuration
docs.text: Variable Flag Description GOOGLE_CREDENTIALS_FILE --credentials Path to Google service account JSON GOAUTH_CREDENTIALS_FILE --goauth-credentials-file Path to goauth CredentialsSet JSON GOAUTH_CREDENTIALS_ACCOUNT --goauth-credentials-account Account key in CredentialsSet OMNITOKEN_VAULT_URI --vault Vault URI for credentials OMNITOKEN_CREDENTIALS_NAME --credentials-name Name of credentials in vault

docs.title: Vault Provider Authentication
docs.text: Variable Description OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN 1Password service account token BW_ACCESS_TOKEN Bitwarden access token BW_ORGANIZATION_ID Bitwarden organization ID

docs.title: Precedence
docs.text:
Command-line flags take precedence over environment variables.

# Environment variable is used
export GOOGLE_CREDENTIALS_FILE=/path/to/env-creds.json
mcp-google
# Uses: /path/to/env-creds.json

# Flag overrides environment
export GOOGLE_CREDENTIALS_FILE=/path/to/env-creds.json
mcp-google --credentials /path/to/flag-creds.json
# Uses: /path/to/flag-creds.json

docs.title: Google Service Account
docs.text:
export GOOGLE_CREDENTIALS_FILE=/path/to/service-account.json
mcp-google

docs.title: goauth CredentialsSet
docs.text:
export GOAUTH_CREDENTIALS_FILE=/path/to/credentials.json
export GOAUTH_CREDENTIALS_ACCOUNT=myaccount
mcp-google

docs.title: Vault-Backed (File)
docs.text:
export OMNITOKEN_VAULT_URI=file:///path/to/secrets
export OMNITOKEN_CREDENTIALS_NAME=google
mcp-google

docs.title: 1Password
docs.text:
export OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN="ops_..."
export OMNITOKEN_VAULT_URI=op://MyVault
export OMNITOKEN_CREDENTIALS_NAME=google
mcp-google

docs.title: Bitwarden
docs.text:
export BW_ACCESS_TOKEN="..."
export BW_ORGANIZATION_ID="..."
export OMNITOKEN_VAULT_URI=bw://org-id
export OMNITOKEN_CREDENTIALS_NAME=google
mcp-google

docs.title: Bash/Zsh
docs.text:
Add to ~/.bashrc or ~/.zshrc:

# Google MCP Server credentials
export GOOGLE_CREDENTIALS_FILE="$HOME/.config/google-mcp/service-account.json"

docs.title: Fish
docs.text:
Add to ~/.config/fish/config.fish:

set -gx GOOGLE_CREDENTIALS_FILE "$HOME/.config/google-mcp/service-account.json"

docs.title: Dotenv Files
docs.text:
For project-specific configuration, use a .env file:

# .env
GOOGLE_CREDENTIALS_FILE=/path/to/service-account.json

Load with source or a dotenv tool:

source .env
mcp-google

Or use a tool like direnv for automatic loading.

docs.title: Docker
docs.text:
FROM golang:1.24 AS builder
# ... build steps ...

FROM alpine:latest
COPY --from=builder /app/mcp-google /usr/local/bin/
ENV GOOGLE_CREDENTIALS_FILE=/secrets/service-account.json
CMD ["mcp-google"]

Run with mounted secrets:

docker run -v /path/to/secrets:/secrets mcp-google

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Go 1.24+
  • Google Cloud service account with Slides and Docs API access

docs.title: Install from Source
docs.text: go install github.com/grokify/mcp-google/cmd/mcp-google@latest

docs.title: Build from Source
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/grokify/mcp-google.git
cd mcp-google
go build ./cmd/mcp-google

docs.title: Verify Installation
docs.text:
mcp-google version
# Output: mcp-google v0.4.0

docs.title: Using as a Library
docs.text:
To use the Google skills in your own omniskill-based server:

go get github.com/grokify/mcp-google@latest

Then import the skills:

import (
    "github.com/grokify/mcp-google/skills/slides"
    "github.com/grokify/mcp-google/skills/docs"
)

See Using Skills for details.

docs.title: With Google Service Account
docs.text: mcp-google --credentials /path/to/service-account.json

docs.title: With Environment Variable
docs.text:
export GOOGLE_CREDENTIALS_FILE=/path/to/service-account.json
mcp-google

docs.title: Presentations
docs.text:
The presentation ID is in the URL:

https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/PRESENTATION_ID_HERE/edit

docs.title: Documents
docs.text:
The document ID is in the URL:

https://docs.google.com/document/d/DOCUMENT_ID_HERE/edit

URLs Supported

Google Docs tools accept either the document ID or the full URL, including URLs with query strings and anchors: 

https://docs.google.com/document/d/DOCUMENT_ID_HERE/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.xyz

docs.title: Example Tool Calls
docs.text: Once the server is running with an MCP client, you can call tools:

docs.title: Get Presentation Metadata
docs.text:
{
  "name": "get_presentation",
  "arguments": {
    "presentation_id": "1BxiMVs0XRA5nFMdKvBdBZjgmUUqptlbs74OgvE2upms"
  }
}

docs.title: Get Document Text
docs.text:
{
  "name": "get_document_text",
  "arguments": {
    "document_id": "https://docs.google.com/document/d/1abc123/edit"
  }
}

docs.title: Get All Slide Content with Notes
docs.text:
{
  "name": "get_presentation_content",
  "arguments": {
    "presentation_id": "1BxiMVs0XRA5nFMdKvBdBZjgmUUqptlbs74OgvE2upms",
    "include_notes": true
  }
}

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Tools Reference - Full documentation for all 9 tools
  • Configuration - All credential options
  • Claude Desktop - Integration with Claude Desktop

docs.title: Create a Google Cloud Service Account
docs.text:
1. Go to the Google Cloud Console
  2. Create a new project or select an existing one
  3. Enable the Google Slides API and Google Docs API:
  4. Go to APIs & Services > Library
  5. Search for "Google Slides API" and enable it
  6. Search for "Google Docs API" and enable it
  7. Create a service account:
  8. Go to APIs & Services > Credentials
  9. Click "Create Credentials" > "Service Account"
  10. Give it a name (e.g., "mcp-server")
  11. No special roles needed for read-only access
  12. Download the JSON credentials file:
  13. Click on the service account
  14. Go to "Keys" tab
  15. Add Key > Create new key > JSON
  16. Save the downloaded file securely

docs.title: Share Documents with the Service Account
docs.text:
The service account email (found in the credentials JSON as client_email) needs access to documents:

  1. Open the Google Slides presentation or Google Doc
  2. Click "Share"
  3. Add the service account email (e.g., mcp-server@your-project.iam.gserviceaccount.com)
  4. Grant "Viewer" access (read-only is sufficient)

Sharing with Google Workspace

If you're using Google Workspace, you can share entire folders or drives with the service account for broader access.

docs.title: Required OAuth Scopes
docs.text:
The server uses these read-only scopes:

  • https://www.googleapis.com/auth/presentations.readonly - Read Slides presentations
  • https://www.googleapis.com/auth/documents.readonly - Read Docs documents
  • https://www.googleapis.com/auth/drive.readonly - Access files shared with the service account

These scopes are automatically configured when using the server.

docs.title: Building Multi-Service MCP Servers
docs.text: This guide shows how to combine Google skills with other services to create a unified MCP server.

docs.title: 1. Define Dependencies
docs.text:
// go.mod
module github.com/example/work-mcp-server

require (
    github.com/grokify/mcp-google v0.4.0
    github.com/plexusone/omniskill v0.1.0
    github.com/plexusone/omnitoken v0.1.0
    // Add other skill packages
)

docs.title: 2. Create Skill Providers
docs.text:
Encapsulate skill creation with authentication:

package providers

import (
    "context"
    "net/http"

    "github.com/grokify/mcp-google/skills/slides"
    "github.com/grokify/mcp-google/skills/docs"
    "github.com/plexusone/omniskill/skill"
)

type GoogleProvider struct {
    client      *http.Client
    slidesSkill *slides.Skill
    docsSkill   *docs.Skill
}

func NewGoogleProvider(client *http.Client) *GoogleProvider {
    return &GoogleProvider{client: client}
}

func (p *GoogleProvider) Init(ctx context.Context) error {
    p.slidesSkill = slides.New(p.client)
    if err := p.slidesSkill.Init(ctx); err != nil {
        return err
    }

    p.docsSkill = docs.New(p.client)
    if err := p.docsSkill.Init(ctx); err != nil {
        return err
    }

    return nil
}

func (p *GoogleProvider) Skills() []skill.Skill {
    return []skill.Skill{p.slidesSkill, p.docsSkill}
}

func (p *GoogleProvider) Close() error {
    _ = p.slidesSkill.Close()
    _ = p.docsSkill.Close()
    return nil
}

docs.title: 3. Configure Credentials
docs.text:
Use omnitoken for unified credential management:

package auth

import (
    "context"
    "net/http"

    "github.com/plexusone/omnitoken"
)

type Credentials struct {
    mgr *omnitoken.TokenManager
}

func NewCredentials(vaultURI string) (*Credentials, error) {
    mgr, err := omnitoken.NewFromVaultURI(vaultURI)
    if err != nil {
        return nil, err
    }
    return &Credentials{mgr: mgr}, nil
}

func (c *Credentials) GoogleClient(ctx context.Context) (*http.Client, error) {
    return c.mgr.GetClient(ctx, "google")
}

func (c *Credentials) SlackClient(ctx context.Context) (*http.Client, error) {
    return c.mgr.GetClient(ctx, "slack")
}

func (c *Credentials) JiraClient(ctx context.Context) (*http.Client, error) {
    return c.mgr.GetClient(ctx, "jira")
}

func (c *Credentials) Close() error {
    return c.mgr.Close()
}

docs.title: 4. Main Server
docs.text:
package main

import (
    "context"
    "log"
    "os"

    "github.com/example/work-mcp-server/auth"
    "github.com/example/work-mcp-server/providers"
    "github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk/mcp"
    runtime "github.com/plexusone/omniskill/mcp/server"
)

func main() {
    ctx := context.Background()

    // Initialize credentials
    creds, err := auth.NewCredentials(os.Getenv("VAULT_URI"))
    if err != nil {
        log.Fatal(err)
    }
    defer creds.Close()

    // Create runtime
    rt := runtime.New(&mcp.Implementation{
        Name:    "work-mcp-server",
        Version: "v1.0.0",
    }, nil)

    // Initialize Google provider
    googleClient, _ := creds.GoogleClient(ctx)
    googleProvider := providers.NewGoogleProvider(googleClient)
    if err := googleProvider.Init(ctx); err != nil {
        log.Fatal(err)
    }
    defer googleProvider.Close()

    // Register Google skills with prefix
    for _, skill := range googleProvider.Skills() {
        rt.RegisterSkillWithPrefix(skill)
    }

    // Add other providers (Slack, Jira, etc.)
    // ...

    // Serve
    if err := rt.ServeStdio(ctx); err != nil {
        log.Fatal(err)
    }
}

docs.title: Strategy 1: Prefixed (Recommended for Multi-Service)
docs.text:
rt.RegisterSkillWithPrefix(slidesSkill)  // slides_get_presentation
rt.RegisterSkillWithPrefix(docsSkill)    // docs_get_document
rt.RegisterSkillWithPrefix(slackSkill)   // slack_send_message

docs.title: Strategy 2: No Prefix (When Tool Names Don't Conflict)
docs.text:
rt.RegisterSkill(slidesSkill)  // get_presentation
rt.RegisterSkill(docsSkill)    // get_document
rt.RegisterSkill(slackSkill)   // send_message

docs.title: Strategy 3: Custom Tool Names
docs.text:
Build wrapper skills with custom tool names:

type CustomGoogleSkill struct {
    slides *slides.Skill
    docs   *docs.Skill
}

func (s *CustomGoogleSkill) Tools() []skill.Tool {
    // Create tools with custom names
    return []skill.Tool{
        skill.NewTool("google_slides_read", "...", params, handler),
        skill.NewTool("google_docs_fetch", "...", params, handler),
    }
}

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
# Vault URI for all credentials
export VAULT_URI=file:///path/to/secrets

# Or individual credential names
export GOOGLE_CREDS_NAME=google
export SLACK_CREDS_NAME=slack
export JIRA_CREDS_NAME=jira

docs.title: Claude Desktop Config
docs.text:
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "work": {
      "command": "/path/to/work-mcp-server",
      "env": {
        "VAULT_URI": "file:///path/to/secrets"
      }
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Graceful Degradation
docs.text:
If one service fails to initialize, you may want to continue with others:

// Try Google
googleClient, err := creds.GoogleClient(ctx)
if err != nil {
    log.Printf("Warning: Google not available: %v", err)
} else {
    googleProvider := providers.NewGoogleProvider(googleClient)
    if err := googleProvider.Init(ctx); err != nil {
        log.Printf("Warning: Google init failed: %v", err)
    } else {
        for _, skill := range googleProvider.Skills() {
            rt.RegisterSkillWithPrefix(skill)
        }
    }
}

// Continue with Slack, Jira, etc.

docs.title: Health Checks
docs.text:
Consider adding a health check tool:

func healthCheckTool(providers map[string]bool) skill.Tool {
    return skill.NewTool(
        "health_check",
        "Check which services are available",
        nil,
        func(ctx context.Context, params map[string]any) (any, error) {
            return providers, nil
        },
    )
}

docs.title: Using Skills
docs.text: The Google Slides and Docs skills can be imported into any omniskill-based MCP server.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: go get github.com/grokify/mcp-google@latest

docs.title: Import
docs.text:
import (
    "github.com/grokify/mcp-google/skills/slides"
    "github.com/grokify/mcp-google/skills/docs"
)

docs.title: Slides Skill
docs.text:
func slides.New(httpClient *http.Client) *slides.Skill

Creates a new Slides skill. The HTTP client must be authenticated with Google OAuth credentials that have the presentations.readonly scope.

docs.title: Docs Skill
docs.text:
func docs.New(httpClient *http.Client) *docs.Skill

Creates a new Docs skill. The HTTP client must be authenticated with Google OAuth credentials that have the documents.readonly scope.

docs.title: 1. Create
docs.text:
slidesSkill := slides.New(httpClient)
docsSkill := docs.New(httpClient)

docs.title: 2. Initialize
docs.text:
if err := slidesSkill.Init(ctx); err != nil {
    return fmt.Errorf("failed to initialize Slides skill: %w", err)
}

if err := docsSkill.Init(ctx); err != nil {
    return fmt.Errorf("failed to initialize Docs skill: %w", err)
}

Init() creates the Google API client. It must be called before registering with a runtime.

docs.title: 3. Register
docs.text:
rt.RegisterSkill(slidesSkill)
rt.RegisterSkill(docsSkill)

docs.title: 4. Close
docs.text:
defer slidesSkill.Close()
defer docsSkill.Close()

Close() is currently a no-op but should be called for forward compatibility.

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text: The skills require an authenticated *http.Client. Options include:

docs.title: Using omnitoken
docs.text:
import "github.com/plexusone/omnitoken"

// From service account file
mgr, _ := omnitoken.NewFromFile("/path/to/service-account.json")
client, _ := mgr.GetClient(ctx, "google")

docs.title: Using Google's oauth2 package
docs.text:
import "golang.org/x/oauth2/google"

data, _ := os.ReadFile("/path/to/service-account.json")
config, _ := google.JWTConfigFromJSON(data,
    "https://www.googleapis.com/auth/presentations.readonly",
    "https://www.googleapis.com/auth/documents.readonly",
)
client := config.Client(ctx)

docs.title: Using goauth
docs.text:
import "github.com/grokify/goauth"

client, _ := goauth.NewClient(ctx, "/path/to/credentials.json", "account-key")

docs.title: Required Scopes
docs.text:
Both skills require these scopes:

Scope Purpose presentations.readonly Read Slides presentations documents.readonly Read Docs documents drive.readonly Access files shared with service account

docs.title: Slides Skill (Name() = "slides")
docs.text: Tool Description get_presentation Get presentation metadata list_slides List all slides get_slide Get single slide content get_slide_notes Get speaker notes get_presentation_content Get all content

docs.title: Docs Skill (Name() = "docs")
docs.text: Tool Description get_document Get document metadata get_document_content Get structured content get_document_text Get plain text get_document_paragraphs Get text by paragraphs

docs.title: Complete Example
docs.text:
package main

import (
    "context"
    "log"

    "github.com/grokify/mcp-google/skills/slides"
    "github.com/grokify/mcp-google/skills/docs"
    "github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk/mcp"
    "github.com/plexusone/omnitoken"
    runtime "github.com/plexusone/omniskill/mcp/server"
)

func main() {
    ctx := context.Background()

    // Get authenticated client
    mgr, err := omnitoken.NewFromFile("/path/to/service-account.json")
    if err != nil {
        log.Fatal(err)
    }
    defer mgr.Close()

    httpClient, err := mgr.GetClient(ctx, "google")
    if err != nil {
        log.Fatal(err)
    }

    // Create skills
    slidesSkill := slides.New(httpClient)
    if err := slidesSkill.Init(ctx); err != nil {
        log.Fatal(err)
    }
    defer slidesSkill.Close()

    docsSkill := docs.New(httpClient)
    if err := docsSkill.Init(ctx); err != nil {
        log.Fatal(err)
    }
    defer docsSkill.Close()

    // Create and configure runtime
    rt := runtime.New(&mcp.Implementation{
        Name:    "my-google-server",
        Version: "v1.0.0",
    }, nil)

    rt.RegisterSkill(slidesSkill)
    rt.RegisterSkill(docsSkill)

    // Run
    if err := rt.ServeStdio(ctx); err != nil {
        log.Fatal(err)
    }
}

docs.title: Google Docs Tools
docs.text:
URL Support

All Google Docs tools accept either the document ID or the full URL, including URLs with query strings and anchors like: 

https://docs.google.com/document/d/abc123/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.xyz

docs.title: get_document
docs.text: Get metadata about a Google Doc document.

docs.title: Input
docs.text: Parameter Type Required Description document_id string Yes The ID or URL of the Google Doc

docs.title: Output
docs.text: Field Type Description title string Document title document_id string Document ID revision_id string Current revision ID word_count integer Approximate word count char_count integer Character count image_count integer Number of images table_count integer Number of tables header_count integer Number of headers footer_count integer Number of footers

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
{
  "name": "get_document",
  "arguments": {
    "document_id": "1BxiMVs0XRA5nFMdKvBdBZjgmUUqptlbs74OgvE2upms"
  }
}

docs.title: get_document_content
docs.text: Get the full structured content of a document including headings, paragraphs, and optionally images and tables.

docs.title: Input
docs.text: Parameter Type Required Description document_id string Yes The ID or URL of the Google Doc include_images boolean No Include image information (default: false) include_tables boolean No Include table content (default: false) include_headers boolean No Include document headers (default: false) include_footers boolean No Include document footers (default: false)

docs.title: Output
docs.text: Field Type Description title string Document title sections array Array of content sections sections[].type string Section type ("heading", "paragraph") sections[].level integer Heading level (1-6, for headings only) sections[].text string Section text content sections[].style_id string Style identifier (e.g., "HEADING_1") images array Array of images (if requested) images[].object_id string Image element ID images[].content_uri string Direct URL to image images[].source_uri string Original source URL images[].title string Image title images[].description string Image description tables array Array of tables (if requested) tables[].rows integer Number of rows tables[].columns integer Number of columns tables[].cells array 2D array of cell text content headers array Array of header text (if requested) footers array Array of footer text (if requested)

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
{
  "name": "get_document_content",
  "arguments": {
    "document_id": "https://docs.google.com/document/d/abc123/edit",
    "include_images": true,
    "include_tables": true
  }
}

docs.title: get_document_text
docs.text: Get all text content from a document as a single plain text string.

docs.title: Input
docs.text: Parameter Type Required Description document_id string Yes The ID or URL of the Google Doc

docs.title: Output
docs.text: Field Type Description title string Document title text string Full document text

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
{
  "name": "get_document_text",
  "arguments": {
    "document_id": "1BxiMVs0XRA5nFMdKvBdBZjgmUUqptlbs74OgvE2upms"
  }
}

Best for Simple Text Extraction

Use this when you just need the raw text content without structure.

docs.title: get_document_paragraphs
docs.text: Get text organized by paragraphs.

docs.title: Input
docs.text: Parameter Type Required Description document_id string Yes The ID or URL of the Google Doc

docs.title: Output
docs.text: Field Type Description title string Document title paragraphs array Array of paragraph text strings

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
{
  "name": "get_document_paragraphs",
  "arguments": {
    "document_id": "1BxiMVs0XRA5nFMdKvBdBZjgmUUqptlbs74OgvE2upms"
  }
}

Best for Paragraph-Level Processing

Use this when you need to process text paragraph by paragraph, such as for summarization or analysis tasks.

docs.title: Tools Overview
docs.text: Google MCP Server provides 9 tools for reading Google Slides and Google Docs.

docs.title: Tool Categories
docs.text: Category Tools Description Google Slides 5 tools Read presentations, slides, and speaker notes Google Docs 4 tools Read documents, content, and paragraphs

docs.title: Google Slides
docs.text: Tool Purpose get_presentation Presentation metadata (title, slide count) list_slides List all slides with titles get_slide Single slide content and elements get_slide_notes Speaker notes for a slide get_presentation_content All content in one call (ideal for AI)

docs.title: Google Docs
docs.text: Tool Purpose get_document Document metadata (title, word count) get_document_content Structured content with optional images/tables get_document_text Plain text extraction get_document_paragraphs Text organized by paragraphs

docs.title: AI Analysis of Entire Document
docs.text:
For AI analysis, use the bulk retrieval tools:

  • Slides: get_presentation_content with include_notes: true
  • Docs: get_document_content with all include flags

docs.title: Targeted Retrieval
docs.text:
For specific content:

  • Slides: Use list_slides then get_slide for specific slides
  • Docs: Use get_document_paragraphs for structured text

docs.title: Metadata Only
docs.text:
For quick metadata checks:

  • Slides: get_presentation
  • Docs: get_document

docs.title: get_presentation
docs.text: Get metadata about a Google Slides presentation.

docs.title: Input
docs.text: Parameter Type Required Description presentation_id string Yes The ID of the Google Slides presentation

docs.title: Output
docs.text: Field Type Description title string Presentation title slide_count integer Number of slides locale string Presentation locale revision_id string Current revision ID

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
{
  "name": "get_presentation",
  "arguments": {
    "presentation_id": "1BxiMVs0XRA5nFMdKvBdBZjgmUUqptlbs74OgvE2upms"
  }
}

docs.title: list_slides
docs.text: List all slides in a presentation with their titles and element counts.

docs.title: Input
docs.text: Parameter Type Required Description presentation_id string Yes The ID of the Google Slides presentation

docs.title: Output
docs.text: Field Type Description slides array Array of slide information slides[].object_id string Slide's unique identifier slides[].index integer Zero-based slide index slides[].title string Slide title (if present) slides[].element_count integer Number of elements on the slide

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
{
  "name": "list_slides",
  "arguments": {
    "presentation_id": "1BxiMVs0XRA5nFMdKvBdBZjgmUUqptlbs74OgvE2upms"
  }
}

docs.title: get_slide
docs.text: Get the content and elements of a specific slide.

docs.title: Input
docs.text:
Parameter Type Required Description presentation_id string Yes The ID of the Google Slides presentation slide_index integer No* Zero-based index of the slide slide_object_id string No* Object ID of the slide

*One of slide_index or slide_object_id must be provided.

docs.title: Output
docs.text: Field Type Description index integer Slide index title string Slide title text_content array Array of text strings from the slide element_summary array Array of element details element_summary[].object_id string Element's unique identifier element_summary[].element_type string Type (shape, image, table, etc.) element_summary[].description string Element description or text preview images array Array of images on the slide images[].object_id string Image element ID images[].content_url string Direct URL to image (~30 min validity) images[].source_url string Original source URL images[].alt_text string Image description

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
{
  "name": "get_slide",
  "arguments": {
    "presentation_id": "1BxiMVs0XRA5nFMdKvBdBZjgmUUqptlbs74OgvE2upms",
    "slide_index": 0
  }
}

docs.title: get_slide_notes
docs.text: Get the speaker notes for a specific slide.

docs.title: Input
docs.text:
Parameter Type Required Description presentation_id string Yes The ID of the Google Slides presentation slide_index integer No* Zero-based index of the slide slide_object_id string No* Object ID of the slide

*One of slide_index or slide_object_id must be provided.

docs.title: Output
docs.text: Field Type Description notes string Speaker notes text

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
{
  "name": "get_slide_notes",
  "arguments": {
    "presentation_id": "1BxiMVs0XRA5nFMdKvBdBZjgmUUqptlbs74OgvE2upms",
    "slide_index": 0
  }
}

docs.title: get_presentation_content
docs.text: Get all slide content in a single call - ideal for AI analysis of the entire presentation.

docs.title: Input
docs.text: Parameter Type Required Description presentation_id string Yes The ID of the Google Slides presentation include_notes boolean No Include speaker notes (default: false)

docs.title: Output
docs.text: Field Type Description title string Presentation title slides array Array of slide content slides[].index integer Zero-based slide index slides[].object_id string Slide's unique identifier slides[].title string Slide title (if present) slides[].text_content array Array of text strings slides[].images array Array of images slides[].notes string Speaker notes (if include_notes is true)

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
{
  "name": "get_presentation_content",
  "arguments": {
    "presentation_id": "1BxiMVs0XRA5nFMdKvBdBZjgmUUqptlbs74OgvE2upms",
    "include_notes": true
  }
}

Best for AI Analysis

This tool retrieves everything in one call, minimizing round-trips. Use it when you need to analyze the entire presentation.
